item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gonna preface this by saying that this is a great article.

This makes me viscerally sickened and enraged, not because it's in any way a bad story, quite the opposite. It's a story that is so well written that it can provoke such strong emotions. I'm not sure if this is meant to be satire, but it certainly came off that way to me. I can think of several real world scams that operate similarly in terms of demanding sacrifices from the marks that the people running the scam aren't actually making themselves. Encouraging marks to get their children involved is a feature of many IRL scams too, as is the victim blaming of everyone who's not involved with the scam or who can't get the scam to work for them. Great stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bulk of this article is very good, and I'd like to upvote it on that basis alone.

However, that second picture. Yuck.

There is a fine line between images that inspire fear and images that are just nasty in the same way that finding an un-maintained toilet in a gas station is nasty. That image, in my opinion, is the latter. I think the article would be fine without it.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The youtube picture is really disturbing me because it just seems too much like something I could really see on that terrifying website.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one hell of a cringe-worthy article that doesn't let up from straying from it's disturbing concept as an anomaly.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the definition of "Thanks, I hate it". Winced regularly throughout the whole thing, had to stop reading it a couple times. Amazing article, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you should keep the two addendums. The concept of pulling teeth out and eating them to increase stock value wasn't necessarily horrifying to me. This is an incredibly strange universe, after all, and worse rituals have been seen. It's an amazingly weird article with just the base concept, definitely worthy of upvotes, but the addendums are what bring the theme home along with adding a sense of subtle horror. +1 from me, and a plea to keep the additions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see benefit from the addenda as you say, but they didn't work for me. I wish there were a way to include those ideas another way. Maybe just as 2-3 sentences?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the 2 addenda are pretty good. The carcinoma one brings the horror element by showing that this ritual is not harmless, and the last one shows that even though this Lucien guy actually possesses magical powers, he is still a fraud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. The first incident (with the tumours) felt a little tacked on as nothing like this had been hinted at before hand (I got the "teeth regrowing elsewhere" bit but the tumours being alive and eat the person from the inside was a bit odd). It's a cool visual, but I think it would be stronger if it was hinted at somehow earlier. Otherwise, going from the podcast to the bit about dentures probably would have been punchier.

None the less, this is still great, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I hate it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't ask me where I got all those teeth. Listen, I know a guy.

I think that anyone want to know where you get all those bloody teeth…

+1

Because I like the articles relates with investments

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suspected this was The Great Hippo the moment I saw "I/O-YUZE"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very very very impressive dialogue, completely hooked me in. This SCP 100% earns my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my favorite way of doing the untoothing challange!!

1. accumulate a bunch of teeth overtime

2. get bowl and a jug of milk

3. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah — I made the change. Hopefully this language makes it clearer what's going on:

SCP-4089 is an investment strategy in which the extraction and consumption of teeth produces increased returns on certain stock portfolios.

I didn't want to get too specific here, because the closer you look at this, the more weird it gets — that being said, I think (hope?) this will make it more clear where the money's coming from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that that could be more explicitly stated. With how it’s written now it sounds like the money just arrives in the mail in an unmarked envelope

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I might want to qualify this a little bit more clearly in the skip:

It's an investment scheme. Every time you pull and swallow teeth as per a ritual, your stock portfolio creeps up in value.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The dialouge is so damn manipulative. Really creepy.
I qm kinda confused where the money actually came from though? Or was it completely fake? If it was completely fake, why the anomalous tumors?

Got enough of a reaction out of me for a +1 either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate this. I hate this so, so much. I wish something else could've taken this slot. Maybe an article about kittens. Or even unicorns. But no, we get an article about pulling teeth out. Goddammit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was really tempted to end it at the podcast. The final two addendums were, ultimately, kinda tacked on at the end. Together, they serve two purposes: To establish that this horrible thing has horrible consequences (the teeth aren't the only thing that grows back), and to establish that Lucien Marchand is a hypocrite and a fraud.

I'll give some thought as to whether or not to trim it. I feel like I wanted to go a little over the top for once (rather than something restrained), but if more people feel like the last two addendums aren't needed, I'm very comfortable just junking them. The final line in the log is a pretty good (albeit weird) stinger.

Also, thanks for the compliment! I mention it in the author's post, but the conversation is based on this interview (which inspired the skip).

item.title: i did the untoothing challange AT 3 AM (GOT A TUMOR IN MY STOMACH OMG)
item.content:encoded: i love the way you do real world shit so realistically. something about ur skips are always so real to me and that's why i love them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This scared me in a very visceral, very fearful way. I’ve been in investing communities, and the dialogue between the two fellows was way too familiar to me, and it affected me in a way that it almost feels like this SCP might’ve been written just to scare me. Only a writing genius can do that.

My main complaint lies in the last two addenda, which I didn’t really get a reaction from. The first one has a good picture, but I didn’t get the point. I didn’t get too much of a reaction from the second one either. IMO it could’ve ended after the podcast.

+1 mainly because the podcast made me shit myself

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking Christ, that second picture made me upchuck my nunchucks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: my teeth are itchy now and there's nothing i can do about it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dog. I love and hate the way you're always wilding.

item.title: yummy nummy teeth in my belly
item.content:encoded:
Greed -- one of the seven deadly sins
Many gonna enter, few gonna win
Always try to notify the next of kin --
'Cause many gonna enter, few gonna win

SOURCES:

  • The video streaming UI is something I made myself. It's available here: Link. I hereby release it into the public domain — do with it as you wish!
  • The photo for the video stream is mine (I took it myself). I also release it into public domain.
  • Other images are here and here.
  • Inspired by this repugnant video (some of the lines in the log are directly lifted quotes).
  • Don't ask me where I got all those teeth. Listen, I know a guy.

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!

pop your white collars up pop your white collars up
